strengths: Current role at re:cinq includes hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators — a direct hit on the JD's 'experience with AI agent development' requirement, rare at this specificity.

15+ years of continuous full-stack delivery (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS CDK at Optilyz; Node.js/Kubernetes at GISCollective) far exceeds the 6-year threshold and maps cleanly onto the primary and secondary stacks.

Strong testing culture evidenced across multiple roles (TDD at RIDE Capital, Selenium course authorship, open-source test frameworks Fluent-Asserts and Trial) directly aligns with the JD's emphasis on Vitest/Jest/Playwright and code-quality practices.
concerns: NestJS is listed as a primary framework in the JD but does not appear anywhere in the resume; depth with this specific framework is unverified.
Kotlin/Java exposure is limited to early robotics work at Altom (2016–2018) with no recent signal; the JD lists these as secondary but active requirements.
Most database evidence centres on MongoDB; explicit PostgreSQL or MySQL production experience is sparse and would need probing to confirm relational DB depth.

key_factors.factor: Primary stack alignment (TypeScript, Node.js, React, AWS)

response.justification: Bogdan is a well-rounded, polyglot senior engineer whose current role at re:cinq already covers the two most differentiating requirements of this position — fullstack TypeScript/Node.js product work and active AI-agent/MCP tooling — while his Optilyz tenure demonstrates direct experience with the exact cloud-plus-React-plus-MongoDB combination the JD targets; the NestJS gap is a real unknown but is easily bridgeable for someone with his depth in the Node ecosystem, making him a compelling candidate for the role.
